children.label: A
children.text: applies the fundamental operating principles and procedures in the Federal Acquisition Regulation;

children.label: B
children.text: uses efficient commercial standards of practice; and

children.label: C
children.text: is suitable for adoption and uninterrupted use by the Republic of Panama after the Canal Transfer Date.
The Regulation shall contain provisions regarding the establishment of the Panama Canal Board of Contract Appeals described in .section 3862 of this title

children.heading: Supplement to Regulation
children.label: b Supplement to Regulation
children.text: The Commission shall develop a Supplement to the Regulation (in this section referred to as the “Supplement”) that identifies both the provisions of Federal law applicable to procurement of goods and services by the Commission and the provisions of Federal law waived by the Commission under subsection (c).

Subject to paragraph (2), the Commission shall determine which provisions of Federal law should not apply to procurement by the Commission and may waive those laws for purposes of the Regulation and Supplement.

children.label: B
children.text: chapter 71 of title 41, other than ; orsection 7104(b) of title 41

children.label: C
children.text: civil rights, environmental, or labor laws.

children.heading: Consultation with Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy
children.label: d Consultation with Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy
children.text: In establishing the Regulation and developing the Supplement, the Commission shall consult with the Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: The Regulation and the Supplement shall take effect on the date of publication in the Federal Register, or , whichever is earlier.January 1, 1999
